page_content:
AMST290 Constructing Cultural Mainstreams and Margins in the U.S. (3 Credits)

Examines the construction, operation, and meaning of cultural mainstreams and margins in a range of contexts, spaces, and times

in the U.S. Using a variety of primary sources, research methods, and interdisciplinary scholarship, we will explore how Americans make and assign meaning to cultural mainstreams and margins. We will examine how and why cultural margins and mainstreams shift over time and what their consequences have been for social policies, laws, power relations, and national identity.

Credit Only Granted for. AMST289A or AMST290.
